authoritative_sources.title: 14 CFR Part 21 - Certification Procedures for Products and Articles
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FAA production certification and approval baseline establishes where safety, quality-system, and release boundaries matter for aerospace coordination, preventing unauthorised disposition.

authoritative_sources.title: FAA AC 21-43A, Production Under 14 CFR Part 21
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FAA official guidance defines aerospace production approval, supplier-control, traceability, and nonconformance handling expectations that gate security coordination handoffs.

authoritative_sources.title: 9100 Quality Management Systems - Requirements for Aviation, Space and Defense Organizations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IAQG 9100 defines aerospace quality terminology and evidence practices that this lane uses to normalize artifacts and preserve supplier/control boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Quality-aware coordination terminology and artifact normalization

authoritative_sources.title: Supply Chain Management Handbook
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The handbook is an official IAQG operating reference for supply-chain planning, supplier coordination, and production handoffs where lot-level evidence and supplier controls affect routing.

authoritative_sources.title: 9131 Nonconformance Data Definition and Documentation
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines aerospace nonconformance documentation categories and escalation discipline used for closure and handoff boundaries in this lane.

authoritative_sources.title: DFARS 252.204-7012 Safeguarding Covered Defense Information
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DFARS clause defines mandatory safeguarding and cyber-incident handling duties for covered defense information used to anchor escalation and reporting boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Security incident and evidence handoff boundaries

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-61 Rev. 2, Computer Security Incident Handling Guide
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: A widely recognized public incident-handling framework that informs safe coordination without authorizing incident closure or technical control implementation.

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-171 Rev. 2, Protecting CUI in Nonfederal Systems
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines control evidence expectations for CUI and vendor handling that shape what records are sufficient before routing security-related dependencies.

authoritative_sources.title: NASA Systems Engineering Handbook, Section 6.5 Configuration Management
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines configuration-control discipline and formal change ownership required before release claims, useful for coordinating security findings in aerospace programs.

workflow_stages.name: Dependency intake and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Capture security-program request, affected lots/batches/serials, production order context, protective-control scope, supplier and hold records before any routing or closure claim.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: Cross-team coordination and routing
workflow_stages.description: Normalize incident, quality, supplier, and logistics terminology; map ownership across adjacent specialists; prepare handoff outputs only with explicit artifact completeness and handoff state.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: Traceability and release-boundary preservation
workflow_stages.description: Preserve lot/serial inventory evidence and release or hold boundaries when conflicts, substitutions, or security events appear, escalating instead of guessing.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: Closure and orchestrator return
workflow_stages.description: Emit closure only with destination-owner acceptance and complete artifact list; otherwise return for escalation or human resolution when sources or policies conflict.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

artifacts: security-program request, incident IDs, severity class, and containment status
protective-control records, security control scope statements, incident/ticket references, and handling plans
production orders, serials, tails, lot/batch IDs, traveler/route, and router/build status
inventory and movement records from ERP, MES, WMS, including quarantine and transfer states
quality hold, NCR, MRB, deviation, waiver, and release records
supplier records, supplier-security declarations, substitute-approval status, and CAPA responses
handoff summaries, dependency trackers, routing confirmations, closure confirmations, and escalation notes
systems_of_record: configuration management or product data management

allowed: classify requests as in-scope, out-of-scope, blocked, or routed
assemble handoff package, tracker, blocker log, and routing package for security-program dependencies
coordinate with adjacent owners while preserving quality/security/release and supplier-control boundaries
escalate: critical ambiguity or conflict in tenant authority, source-of-truth records, or policy
supplier security or nonconformance that requires supplier quality, MRB, engineering, or security lead review
quality, security hold, or release-boundary conditions that block handoff
refuse: bypass traceability, quality hold, supplier-control, release, or legal/security boundaries
approve or execute substitution/mitigation decisions requiring external authority
close incident, hold, or release state without destination-owner proof

domain_failure_modes: A handoff proceeds without authoritative incident, lot, inventory, supplier, or release records.
Conflicting records across ERP, MES, WMS, and supplier systems are collapsed into a confident routing without blocker handling.
Hold, NCR, MRB, or security-tagged material is treated as clear for production movement.
Supplier substitution or supplier-security change is approved without procurement/supplier quality or security authority.
Direct execution control changes are performed in-lane rather than routed to adjacent execution owners.
Closure is declared without destination-owner acceptance and artifact completeness.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Supplier or substitution decision outside lane authority
Work is handed off without required context or artifacts
Multiple teams appear to own the same step
The lane is asked to absorb execution work instead of coordinating it
Ownership is ambiguous across multiple teams
A handoff is attempted without the required context

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific security architecture and CCB/incident authority matrix
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Program- and tenant-specific escalation paths for incident severity, classified material, and release authorization are required at runtime and cannot be inferred from public sources alone.
Public sources do not establish every tenant program’s export-control and classified handling rule set; those are mandatory tenant/runtime inputs.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Security hold nomenclature and release-state mapping
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Tenant systems may use different hold names and release states; mapping is retrieved from tenant policy and governance records.
